questions.question: Explain the three phases of PPP connection establishment.
keyPoints: Phase 1: Link Establishment - LCP configures and tests the link
Phase 2: Authentication (optional) - PAP or CHAP verifies credentials
Phase 3: Network Layer Protocol - NCP (like IPCP) configures network layer parameters

questions.question: Differentiate between PAP and CHAP authentication protocols.
keyPoints: PAP sends passwords in plaintext (less secure), CHAP uses MD5 hashing
PAP uses 2-way handshake, CHAP uses 3-way challenge-response
CHAP can periodically re-authenticate during session
PAP is susceptible to replay attacks, CHAP is more resistant

questions.question: Describe the PPP frame format with all fields.
keyPoints: Flag (0x7E) - marks frame boundaries
Address (0xFF) - broadcast address
Control (0x03) - UI frame
Protocol field - identifies encapsulated protocol
Information - actual data
FCS - error detection
Flag (0x7E) - end marker

questions.question: What are the functions of LCP in PPP?
keyPoints: Link establishment and configuration
Link testing and quality testing
Negotiating options like MRU, authentication
Link termination
Loop detection capability

questions.question: Explain why PPP is preferred over HDLC for point-to-point connections.
keyPoints: HDLC supports only one network layer protocol, PPP supports multiple via Protocol field
PPP has built-in authentication mechanisms (PAP/CHAP)
PPP allows dynamic address assignment via NCP
PPP provides better error detection with configurable FCS
PPP works on both synchronous and asynchronous links

questions.question: What is the role of NCP in PPP? Give examples.
keyPoints: NCP (Network Control Programs) negotiate network layer parameters
IPCP negotiates IP addresses, DNS, and IP-related options
IPXCP negotiates IPX parameters
AppleTalkCP negotiates AppleTalk parameters
Multiple NCPs can run simultaneously for different protocols

questions.question: Draw and explain the complete PPP frame structure. Also explain character escaping mechanism used in PPP.
keyPoints: Draw frame: Flag-Address-Control-Protocol-Information-FCS-Flag
Explain each field purpose and size
Explain byte stuffing: when flag character (0x7E) appears in data, transmit as 0x7D 0x5E
Explain escape sequence: 0x7D is transmitted as 0x7D 0x5D
Explain how control characters below 0x20 are also escaped
This ensures flag sequence is not duplicated within frame

questions.question: Compare PAP and CHAP authentication in detail. Which one would you recommend for secure connections and why?
keyPoints: PAP: 2-way handshake, plaintext password transmission, less secure, simple implementation
CHAP: 3-way handshake, MD5 hashing, challenge-response mechanism, periodic re-authentication
CHAP advantages: resistant to replay attacks, credentials not sent over line, periodic verification
Recommendation: CHAP is preferred for secure connections as it never transmits actual passwords
PAP may be used in low-security environments or for compatibility

questions.question: Explain the complete PPP connection establishment process with state diagram approach.
keyPoints: Initial state: Link Dead - physical layer not ready
Link Establishment phase: LCP Configure-Request sent, Configure-Ack received
Authentication phase (optional): PAP/CHAP exchange
Network Layer Protocol phase: NCP (IPCP) negotiate parameters
Opened state: Data transfer possible
Link Termination phase: LCP Terminate-Request/Response
Explain each LCP packet type: Configure, Terminate, Echo, Discard-Request

questions.question: What are the advantages of PPP over HDLC? Explain the Protocol field in PPP frame with examples.
keyPoints: PPP supports multiple network layer protocols via Protocol field (HDLC doesn't)
PPP has built-in authentication (PAP/CHAP)
PPP allows dynamic address negotiation
PPP works on both async and sync links
Protocol field examples: 0x0021=IP, 0xC021=LCP, 0x8021=IPCP, 0xC023=PAP, 0xC223=CHAP
Explain odd/even protocol field rule for extension
